title: Grammatical case
content: Grammatical case is a linguistics term regarding a manner of categorizing noun s, pronoun s, adjective s, participle s, and numeral s according to their traditionally corresponding grammatical function s within a given phrase, clause, or sentence. In some languages, nouns, pronouns, adjectives, determiner s, participles, prepositions, numerals, articles and their modifiers take different inflected forms, depending on their case. As a language evolves, cases can merge (for instance, in Ancient Greek, the locative case merged with the dative case), a phenomenon formally called syncretism.
